title: Mithranandapuram Vamanamoorthy Temple
tagline: Mithranandapuram Vamanamoorthy Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Mithranandapuram Vamanamoorthy Temple is a Hindu temple situated in Cherpu of Thrissur District of Kerala, India.
overview.description: Mithranandapuram Vamanamoorthy Temple is a Hindu temple situated in Cherpu of Thrissur District of Kerala, India. According to the legends, the idol worshiped here is more than 1,500 years old.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Chevoor
topPlaces.description: Chevoor is a census town in Thrissur district in the Indian state of Kerala. Chevvoor, or Chovvur as it is called in the Indian postal department, is 9 km from Thrissur city on the Thrissur-Irinjalakuda/Kodungallor/Thriprayar route.

topPlaces.name: Thiruvullakkavu Sree Dharma Sastha Temple
topPlaces.description: The Thuiruvullakkavu Sree Dharma Sastha Temple is a Hindu temple located in Cherpu of Thrissur district of Kerala. The deity is Lord Dharma Shastha in standing posture with arch and a bow.

topPlaces.name: Peruvanam
topPlaces.description: Peruvanam (Peruvanam Gramam) is a historical area of Brahmin settlement in central Kerala. In Kerala Sanskrit literature, the name "Peruvanam" is often Sanskritized as Puruvana meaning the forest where a sage named Puru lived and meditated.

topPlaces.name: Peruvanam Mahadeva Temple
topPlaces.description: Peruvanam Mahadev Temple (Malayalam: പെരുവനം മഹാദേവക്ഷേത്രം) is a Shiva Temple situated in Peruvanam of Thrissur District, Kerala, India. The temple is said to be in existence with the origin of Kerala and the advent of Parashurama.

topPlaces.name: Cherpu
topPlaces.description: Cherpu is a suburb of Thrissur city in the Kerala State of South India. It is 12 kilometres south of Thrissur town and is on the Thriprayar road.

topPlaces.name: Paralam
topPlaces.description: Paralam is a village in Thrissur district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Venginissery
topPlaces.description: Venginissery is a suburb in Thrissur district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Urakam, Thrissur
topPlaces.description: Urakam or Oorakam is a neighbourhood near Cherpu in Thrissur of Kerala state in the peninsular India.

items.note: Chevoor is a census town in Thrissur district in the Indian state of Kerala. Chevvoor, or Chovvur as it is called in the Indian postal department, is 9 km from Thrissur city on the Thrissur-Irinjalakuda/Kodungallor/Thriprayar route.
The Thuiruvullakkavu Sree Dharma Sastha Temple is a Hindu temple located in Cherpu of Thrissur district of Kerala. The deity is Lord Dharma Shastha in standing posture with arch and a bow.

Peruvanam (Peruvanam Gramam) is a historical area of Brahmin settlement in central Kerala. In Kerala Sanskrit literature, the name "Peruvanam" is often Sanskritized as Puruvana meaning the forest where a sage named Puru lived and meditated.

Peruvanam Mahadev Temple (Malayalam: പെരുവനം മഹാദേവക്ഷേത്രം) is a Shiva Temple situated in Peruvanam of Thrissur District, Kerala, India. The temple is said to be in existence with the origin of Kerala and the advent of Parashurama.
Cherpu is a suburb of Thrissur city in the Kerala State of South India. It is 12 kilometres south of Thrissur town and is on the Thriprayar road.
Paralam is a village in Thrissur district in the state of Kerala, India.
Venginissery is a suburb in Thrissur district in the state of Kerala, India.
Urakam or Oorakam is a neighbourhood near Cherpu in Thrissur of Kerala state in the peninsular India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Mithranandapuram Vamanamoorthy Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Mithranandapuram Vamanamoorthy Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Mithranandapuram Vamanamoorthy Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (39 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (54 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Mithranandapuram Vamanamoorthy Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Mithranandapuram Vamanamoorthy Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Mithranandapuram Vamanamoorthy Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Mithranandapuram Vamanamoorthy Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Mithranandapuram Vamanamoorthy Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Chevoor, Thiruvullakkavu Sree Dharma Sastha Temple, Peruvanam, Peruvanam Mahadeva Temple, Cherpu.
